questions.question: All living organisms are made of one or more ________, which are the basic units of life.
questions.explanation: The cell is the fundamental structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Some organisms are unicellular (one cell), while others are multicellular (many cells).

questions.question: The sum of all chemical reactions in an organism that provide energy for life processes is called ________.
questions.explanation: Metabolism includes all the chemical reactions that occur within an organism, including both those that break down molecules to release energy (catabolism) and those that build up molecules (anabolism).

questions.question: The ability of an organism to maintain a stable internal environment despite changes in the external environment is called ________.
questions.explanation: Homeostasis refers to the maintenance of a constant internal state, such as body temperature, pH, or blood sugar levels, even when the external environment fluctuates.

questions.question: Organisms grow and increase in complexity through processes guided by ________.
questions.explanation: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) contains the genetic instructions that control growth, development, and function. It directs how an organism increases in size and complexity.

questions.question: The production of offspring, either sexually or asexually, is referred to as ________.
questions.explanation: Reproduction is the biological process by which new individual organisms are produced. It can be asexual (one parent) or sexual (two parents).

questions.question: When an organism detects and responds to changes in its environment, such as moving toward light or away from heat, it is demonstrating ________.
questions.explanation: Response to stimuli (or irritability) is the ability of an organism to react to external or internal signals. Moving toward light (phototaxis) is a classic example.

questions.question: Over many generations, populations of organisms can change traits to better survive in their environment, a process called ________.
questions.explanation: Adaptation (or evolution over time) refers to the process by which populations of organisms change over generations to become better suited to their environment through natural selection.

questions.question: Bacteria reproducing by splitting into two identical cells is an example of ________ reproduction.
questions.explanation: Binary fission is a form of asexual reproduction where a single organism divides into two genetically identical daughter cells. It is common in bacteria and other prokaryotes.

questions.question: A plant growing toward sunlight is responding to an environmental ________.
questions.explanation: Sunlight acts as a stimulus (an external signal). The plant's growth response toward the light is called phototropism, which is a type of response to stimuli.

questions.question: Maintaining water balance in freshwater fish by excreting dilute urine and absorbing salts through gills is an example of ________.
questions.explanation: Freshwater fish constantly take in water and lose salts. Their bodies maintain internal balance (osmoregulation) by excreting excess water and actively absorbing salts, which is a key example of homeostasis.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong understanding of the characteristics of life.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the differences between metabolism, homeostasis, and response to stimuli.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the definitions of each characteristic of life.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the seven characteristics of living things carefully.
